calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jun-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No Appearances.

PAWNEE LEASING CORPORATION's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PAWNEE LEASING CORPORATION's CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is denied. Defendant failed to meet his burden of proof (CCP 703.580(b).) The claim is not credible given the discrepancy between defendant's claimed expenses and claimed income and the assertion that defendant has no cash, bank accounts, cars, equity, or other personal property.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-18-2023 Continued To Jun-06-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

PLAINTIFF PAWNEE LEASING CORPORATION'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is continued to June 6, 2023, on the court's motion.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-29-2021 Off Calendar

Motion For Default Judgment And Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion; Notice Of Motion For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-06-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants 396 Harrison LLC, DBA Nomo, Thaddeus Milstead, a/k/a Thad Vogler, Agriole Group LLC and Agricole II LLC.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Amanda Ferns, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Pawnee Leasing Corporation.
Kenny Fitzgerald, Vice President of Pawnee Leasing Corporation, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Ms. Ferns and Mr. Fitzgerald appeared via ZOOM for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants 396 Harrison LLC, DBA Nomo, Agriole Group LLC or Agricole II LLC.
Defendant Thaddeus Milstead, a/k/a Thad Vogler, was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Lease and Breach of Guaranty was filed on December 7, 2020, and personally served on defendants 396 Harrison LLC, DBA Nomo, Thaddeus Milstead, a/k/a Thad Vogler, Agriole Group LLC and Agricole II LLC on January 7, 2021.
Default as to all four defendants was entered on March 3, 2021.
DOE defendants were dismissed on March 3, 2021.

Plaintiff Pawnee Leasing Corporation submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Lease Agreement signed 12/18/2018..........................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Equipment Lease Corporate Guaranty.........................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 3: Assignment of Lease signed 10/5/2018.......................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 4: Lease Addendum signed 1/4/2019.............................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 5: UCC Financing Statement dated 1/22/2019....................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 6: Notice of Intent to Sell Letter to 396 Harrison LLC dated 7/29/2020........ID/EVID
Exhibit 7: Notice of Intent to Sell Letter to Thaddeus Vogler dated 7/29/2020.........ID/EVID
Exhibit 8: Notice of Intent to Sell Letter to Agricole Group, LLC dated 7/29/2020.....ID/EVID
Exhibit 9: Notice of Intent to Sell Letter to Agricole II LLC dated 7/29/2020.........ID/EVID
Exhibit 10: Copy of check made payable to Pawnee Leasing for $14,877.50...............ID/EVID
Exhibit 11: Asset Recovery Services Auction Payment Report dated 9/0/2020.............ID/EVID
Exhibit 12: Pawnee Leasing Corporation Payoff Quotation dated 11/10/2020..............ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Pawnee Leasing Corporation, and against the defaulted defendants 396 Harrison LLC, DBA Nomo, Thaddeus Milstead, a/k/a Thad Vogler, Agriole Group LLC and Agricole II LLC, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $92,622.20.
Prejudgment Interest - $2,587.74.
Attorney Fees - $3,815.00.
Costs - $730.00.

Total - $99,754.94.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.
